name: Apricot Bread Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cut or tear bread into pieces and place on a baking sheet. Toast the bread pieces in the oven for about 15 minutes.
3. Place the toasted bread in a 2-quart buttered casserole dish.
4. In a large bowl, mix together 3 cups milk, 1 cup cream, 3 eggs, and 1 1/2 cups sugar until well combined.
5. Pour the milk mixture over the bread in the casserole dish and let it absorb the liquid for about 15 minutes.
6. Add almond extract, chopped dried apricots, raisins, and slivered almonds to the casserole dish and mix well with the ingredients.
7. Place the casserole dish inside a larger baking dish. Pour boiling water into the larger dish so that it covers the casserole dish by about an inch.
8. Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour, ensuring there is always enough water in the larger dish to prevent burning.
9. In a saucepan, mix together powdered sugar, margarine, and egg until just come to a boil. Remove from heat and stir in the amaretto.
10. Serve the warm pudding topped with the prepared sauce.
